name: Holiday Cheddar Date Cake
recipeIngredient: 16 ounces Dates, pitted and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 300 degrees F. Grease and flour a 10-inch tube pan.
2. In a large bowl, beat butter and brown sugar with an electric mixer on medium until well blended.
3. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
4. Beat in shredded Cheddar cheese.
5. Sift flour, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, and cloves into a separate bowl.
6. In another bowl, combine dates, pecans, cherries, and raisins with 2 tablespoons of the flour mixture.
7. Mix lightly to coat the fruit and nuts.
8. Alternately beat remaining flour mixture and milk into the butter mixture until well blended.
9. Stir in the floured fruit mixture by hand until distributed throughout the batter.
10. Pour into prepared pan.
11. Bake for 2 to 2 1/2 hours at preheated oven until cake pulls away from the side of the pan and springs back when lightly pressed.
12. Cool on a wire rack for 15 minutes, then remove from the pan.
13. Allow to cool completely before storing in an airtight container for up to 6 weeks.
14. Cut into slices and serve.
